en.extract:
The Sunda coucal (Centropus nigrorufus), also known as the Javanese lathe, is a cuckoo species in the family Cuculidae that is endemic to Java, Indonesia. It inhabits mangroves, freshwater swamp forests and grasslands near brackish water. It has been listed as Vulnerable on the IUCN Red List since 1994, as the small population is threatened by habitat destruction and trapping.
It feeds on grasshoppers, ground beetles, moths, geckos, snakes and frogs; it was also observed while picking rice seeds in a paddy field.


References


External links
BirdLife International (2019). "Javan Coucal Centropus nigrorufus".

The '''Sunda coucal''' ('''''Centropus nigrorufus'''''), also known as the Javanese lathe,<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Damayanti |first1=N. |last2=Marhaento |first2=H. |last3=Poedjirahajoe |first3=E. |date=2022-09-01 |title=Evaluation of blocking area management based on ecological and socio-economic sensitivity mapping in Sermo Wildlife Sanctuary, Kulon Progo, Yogyakarta |journal=IOP Conference Series: Earth and Environmental Science |volume=1039 |issue=1 |article-number=012041 |doi=10.1088/1755-1315/1039/1/012041 |issn=1755-1307|doi-access=free |bibcode=2022E&ES.1039a2041D }}</ref> is a [[cuckoo]] species in the family [[Cuculidae]] that is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Java]], Indonesia. It inhabits [[mangrove]]s, [[freshwater swamp forest]]s and grasslands near [[brackish water]]. It has been listed as [[Vulnerable species|Vulnerable]] on the [[IUCN Red List]] since 1994, as the small population is threatened by [[habitat destruction]] and [[trapping]].<ref name="iucn status 14 November 2021" />
It feeds on [[grasshopper]]s, [[ground beetle]]s, [[moth]]s, [[gecko]]s, [[snake]]s and [[frog]]s; it was also observed while picking [[rice]] seeds in a [[paddy field]].<ref>{{cite journal |author=Budi, N.S. |year=2014 |title=Sunda Coucal ''Centropus nigrorufus'' eating young rice seeds |journal=Kukila |volume=17 |issue=2 |pages=152–153 |url=http://kukila.org/index.php/KKL/article/view/414/418}}</ref>
